questions.q: In which kingdom was the Ashoka Vatika, where Sita was held captive?
questions.explanation: The Ashoka Vatika was a grove in Lanka, the island kingdom ruled by Ravana.

questions.q: Which type of tree gave the Ashoka Vatika its name?
questions.explanation: The grove was named for its ashoka trees, beneath which Sita was kept.

questions.q: Who was held captive in the Ashoka Vatika?
questions.explanation: Sita, the wife of Rama, was imprisoned in the Ashoka Vatika after Ravana abducted her.

questions.q: Who abducted Sita and kept her in the Ashoka Vatika?
questions.explanation: Ravana, the demon king of Lanka, abducted Sita and confined her in his grove.

questions.q: What kind of beings guarded Sita in the Ashoka Vatika?
questions.explanation: Sita was watched over by rakshasis, female demons assigned by Ravana to guard her.

questions.q: How did Sita respond to Ravana's threats and blandishments?
options: She asked for time to decide
questions.explanation: Sita steadfastly refused all of Ravana's threats and enticements, remaining faithful to Rama.

questions.q: Which vanara hero first found Sita in the Ashoka Vatika?
questions.explanation: Hanuman discovered Sita seated beneath the trees of the Ashoka Vatika during his search of Lanka.

questions.q: Who was the husband Sita remained faithful to during her captivity?
questions.explanation: Sita remained devoted to her husband Rama throughout her imprisonment in the Ashoka Vatika.

questions.q: What token from Rama did Hanuman show Sita to prove he was a true messenger?
questions.explanation: Hanuman gave Sita Rama's signet ring as a sign that he had genuinely come from Rama.

questions.q: What was the Ashoka Vatika best described as?
questions.explanation: The Ashoka Vatika was a pleasure grove or garden within Ravana's domain in Lanka.

questions.q: Which kindly rakshasi comforted Sita with a dream foretelling Ravana's fall?
questions.explanation: Trijata, a gentle demoness among the guards, reassured Sita with a dream predicting Rama's victory.

questions.q: From where did Hanuman observe Sita before approaching her in the grove?
options: Hidden among the branches of a tree
questions.explanation: Hanuman concealed himself in the branches of a tree, especially a simsapa tree, to watch Sita unseen.

questions.q: What did Ravana give Sita to decide whether she would accept him?
options: A year (with a final period of about two months)
questions.explanation: Ravana set a deadline of roughly a year, threatening Sita that she must yield within the remaining months.

questions.q: Which tree did Sita sit beneath, where Hanuman found her?
questions.explanation: Sita was seated beneath a simsapa tree in the ashoka grove when Hanuman located her.

questions.q: What token did Sita give Hanuman to carry back to Rama?
questions.explanation: Sita gave Hanuman her chudamani, a precious hair ornament, as a sign for Rama.

questions.q: What did Hanuman offer to do for Sita, which she declined?
options: To carry her on his back out of Lanka
To burn the grove around her
questions.explanation: Hanuman offered to carry Sita away on his back, but she refused, wanting Rama himself to rescue her.

questions.q: Why did Sita refuse Hanuman's offer to take her away from the grove?
options: She wished Rama to win her back and uphold his honor
Ravana had bound her with chains
questions.explanation: Sita declined so that Rama could rescue her himself and restore his honor by defeating Ravana.

questions.q: After meeting Sita, what destructive act did Hanuman carry out involving the grove?
options: He uprooted the ashoka trees and wrecked the garden
He freed wild animals into it
He set fire to the trees with a torch
questions.explanation: Hanuman tore up the ashoka grove, destroying its trees to provoke Ravana's forces.

questions.q: Which despairing thought did Sita entertain while in the Ashoka Vatika?
options: Ending her own life out of grief
questions.explanation: Overcome by grief, Sita contemplated ending her life before Hanuman arrived and gave her hope.

questions.q: Which of Ravana's relatives is traditionally said to be Trijata's father?
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that Trijata, the kindly rakshasi who comforted Sita, was a daughter of Vibhishana.

questions.q: What did the rakshasi guards mainly try to do to Sita to break her resolve?
options: Offer her a throne in Ayodhya
questions.explanation: The rakshasi guards used threats and intimidation, pressuring Sita to submit to Ravana.

questions.q: In the Valmiki Ramayana, the search of Lanka that led to the grove occurs in which kanda (book)?
questions.explanation: Hanuman's leap to Lanka and discovery of Sita in the grove are narrated in the Sundara Kanda.

questions.q: What is another common name used for the Ashoka grove where Sita was kept?
questions.explanation: The grove is also called the Ashoka Vana, simply meaning the forest or garden of ashoka trees.

questions.q: Before Hanuman revealed himself, in what form did he recount Rama's story so Sita would trust him?
options: Speaking softly of Rama's deeds from the tree
questions.explanation: Hidden in the tree, Hanuman gently narrated Rama's history aloud so Sita would not be frightened and would believe him.

questions.q: Which earlier event between Rama and Sita did she ask Hanuman to confirm as proof of his authenticity?
questions.explanation: Sita recalled the crow incident, where Rama used a blade of grass as a weapon, as a private memory only the real Rama would know.

questions.q: When Ravana approached Sita in the grove, what did she place between herself and him as a barrier?
questions.explanation: Sita placed a blade of grass before her, refusing to even address Ravana directly while rebuking him.

questions.q: Which queen of Ravana is sometimes said to have counseled him to return Sita rather than keep her captive?
questions.explanation: Mandodari, Ravana's chief queen, is traditionally portrayed as advising him to give Sita back to Rama.

questions.q: After Hanuman's destruction in the grove, which of Ravana's sons was sent and captured Hanuman with a divine weapon?
questions.explanation: Indrajit, also called Meghanada, bound Hanuman using the brahmastra after lesser warriors failed.

questions.q: Which of Ravana's young sons was slain by Hanuman during the fighting that followed the grove's destruction?
questions.explanation: Hanuman killed Akshayakumara, a son of Ravana, before being captured by Indrajit.

questions.q: What reassurance did Hanuman give Sita about Rama's intentions before he departed Lanka?
options: That Rama would come with an army to rescue her
questions.explanation: Hanuman assured Sita that Rama would soon arrive with his vanara army to defeat Ravana and free her.
